source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A model of a limb consists of soft tissue and bone, as illustrated in Fig. 11.1. The soft tissue has a thickness of 9.0 cm. The bone within the soft tissue has a thickness of 3.0 cm. Data for the linear attenuation (absorption) coefficient $\mu$ of X-rays in soft tissue and in bone are shown in Table 11.1. A parallel beam of X-rays of intensity $I_{0}$ is incident normally on the model. Calculate, in terms of $I_{0}$: the transmitted intensity $I_{\mathrm{S}}$ through soft tissue alone.
extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object string representations, not concrete file paths or URLs
question references Fig. 11.1 and Table 11.1; the table content is not separately provided in the record

decision_reasons: question, answer, and both referenced images are present, so the core semantics are well supported
asset registry passed with no recorded issues or risk flags
high image dependency combined with strong verifiability makes the sample valuable for the candidate pool
